_meta.purpose: Opportunistic per-carrier rating modifiers aligned to the tool's UX inputs. Dated + primary-sourced. Feeds a FUTURE per-carrier P(profile) term; until a factor clears the coverage gate it is a CHECK on the generic curve, not a driver. Full plan in MODIFIERS_PLAN.md.
_meta.gate: Do NOT make P per-carrier for a factor until that factor is captured for >= the top-N carriers a user actually compares in a state (mirrors the drift coverage gate). Below the gate: use as sanity-check/prior only.
_meta.discipline: Grab when a filing already exposes it during serff_filings.json triage. Never a separate hunt, never parse a full manual for it. Missing is fine; half-applied per-carrier is harmful (false ranking differences).

schema.factor: ux slug: young_driver | age_band | homeowner | multi_car | multi_policy | good_student | prior_coverage | credit_tier
schema.value: number or object — discount as signed % (e.g. -0.07) OR a relativity table {band: factor}
schema.prior_value: same shape as value, if the filing shows a CHANGE | null
schema.source_doc: which PDF/section it came from (e.g. 'Rate-Rule: Rule 12 Homeowner Discount')

value._note: homeowner=TRUE factors (non-homeowner=1.00 baseline) by coverage x Prior BI Tier A-D; PROPOSED/current factors from this filing.

prior_value._note: tiers A/B were cut ~-4.5% / -4.3% in this filing; tiers C/D unchanged. Prior (current-before) BI tier-A/B factors shown.

modifiers.source_doc: Exhibit 5 - Homeowner's Discount (Sheets 1-10, GEICO Marine GB)
modifiers.note: Homeowner discount is jointly indexed on homeowner status AND Prior BI Tier (A-D). GEICO Indemnity (GI) in the same filing has its own near-identical table (not separately captured). Non-homeowners = 1.00 (no discount).
